title: Bikes, cameras, and the creative reset
html:
When the Figma file starts blurring together, I grab the bike or the camera. Sometimes both. There's something about the physicality of riding and the intentionality of film that resets the creative brain in a way that scrolling Dribbble never will.

title: Why I keep building side projects
html:
Between Cosmo Studio, my photo site, and this portfolio, I'm always building something on the side. It's not about hustle culture — it's about staying sharp with tools that move fast.

Framer, Ghost, new CSS features — you can read about them or you can ship something with them. I prefer shipping.

title: The restaurant tech design playbook
html:
I've designed ordering experiences for some of the biggest restaurant brands in America. Each one teaches you something different — Dutch Bros moves fast, Denny's has decades of brand equity to respect, and Blaze needs to make customization feel effortless.

The common thread? Users are hungry and impatient. Every tap needs to earn its place.

title: From Cal Poly to San Francisco
html:
I studied Liberal Arts & Engineering at Cal Poly SLO — a degree that sounds made up but turned out to be the perfect foundation for product design. It taught me to think across disciplines, which is basically the job description.

After bouncing through AR headsets at ODG, conversational AI at Botstacks, and clinical software at SureClinical, I landed at Hathway doing restaurant tech — and haven't looked back.

title: Design systems are never finished
html:
A design system isn't a project with a launch date — it's a product with users, bugs, and feature requests. After years of maintaining CarvanaDS, here's what I've learned about keeping a system alive and useful.

The hardest part isn't building components. It's building trust. Teams need to believe the system will be maintained, that their feedback matters, and that adopting it will actually make them faster.

title: Forging the connection between innovation & aesthetic
html:
After 10+ years working across agencies and tech companies, I've found my sweet spot at the intersection of design and engineering. From design systems to front-end code, the best work happens when you stop treating these as separate disciplines.

At Carvana, leading the design system means I'm writing code one hour and pushing pixels the next. It's the kind of role that didn't exist when I started — and it's exactly where I want to be.

title: About this site
html:
Hudson Paine is an independent publication launched in March 2026 by Hudson Paine. If you subscribe today, you'll get full access to the website as well as email newsletters about new content when it's available. Your subscription makes this site possible, and allows Hudson Paine to continue to exist. Thank you!

Access all areas

By signing up, you'll get access to the full archive of everything that's been published before and everything that's still to come. Your very own private library.

Fresh content, delivered

Stay up to date with new content sent straight to your inbox! No more worrying about whether you missed something because of a pesky algorithm or news feed.

Meet people like you

Join a community of other subscribers who share the same interests.

Start your own thing

Enjoying the experience? Get started for free and set up your very own subscription business using Ghost, the same platform that powers this website.
